# Tal Paperin

> Fractional CRO and B2B sales consultant. I rebuild revenue functions and fix broken sales, fast. 30-plus B2B companies rebuilt, $20M ARR managed. Work with me three ways: fractional CRO (me, personally), fully outsourced sales via KSW Solutions, or TheDealMentor.AI for founders who want to run the motion themselves.

## Key pages
- [Tal Paperin, Fractional CRO (home)](https://talpaperin.com/): services, what I fix, case studies, engagements and pricing, contact.
- [Hebrew site](https://talpaperin.com/he/): Hebrew version of the homepage.
- [Blog](https://talpaperin.com/blog/): field notes on B2B sales, fractional CRO leadership, go-to-market and fixing broken revenue.

## Blog posts
- [Fractional CRO vs Full-Time CRO: Which Does Your Company Actually Need?](https://talpaperin.com/blog/fractional-cro-vs-full-time-cro): A practical breakdown of when a fractional CRO beats a full-time hire, what each really costs, and how to tell which one your revenue stage calls for.
- [Leadership requires balls. Most people have let theirs atrophy.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/leadership-requires-balls): Real leadership comes from hardship, not a workshop in Bali. Why clients hire me over a safe full-time hire, and what to do if you lack it.
- [An anti-Israel org targeted me with an ad. It is a masterclass in the wrong ICP.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/an-anti-israel-org-targeted-me-with-an-ad-it): A business consultant. A Fractional CRO. A guy who spends most of his day helping companies improve sales, business development and revenue growth. A religious.
- [Most founders completely misunderstand sales objections.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/most-founders-completely-misunderstand-sales-objections): A while back I was invited by Professor Philip Tran to teach Objection Handling to his students at the College of San Mateo in California. The lecture.
- [Most CEOs don't have a sales problem. They have a motion problem.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/most-ceos-dont-have-a-sales-problem-they-have-a): They have a motion problem. They don't know where to start... And where to go next (and how). Their Salespeople (or they themselves) are calling the wrong.
- [Costco loses money on gas on purpose. Here is the strategy.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/costco-loses-money-on-gas-on-purpose-here-is-the): THAT is the entire strategy. Across 747 stations, Costco makes a few cents per gallon. Regular gas stations make 25 to 35 cents. Lines wrap around their.
- [SentinelOne hit $1B in revenue, then laid off 300 people. Growth is not the goal.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/sentinelone-hit-1b-in-revenue-then-laid-off-300-people): And they're laying off 300 people. Not a startup running out of money. Not a company that can't find customers. Not a company whose product doesn't work. A.
- [I got added to a BDS boycott list. Here is why it helps my business.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/i-got-added-to-a-bds-boycott-list-here-is): A website dedicated to boycotting Israeli companies officially added both me and KSW Solutions to their database. They even gave me my own profile page. After.
- [Nike lost $200B making the mistake I see B2B founders make every quarter.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/nike-lost-200b-making-the-mistake-i-see-b2b-founders): Stock down 75%. They are laying off 1,400 people. The mistake that broke them is one I see B2B founders making every quarter. Smaller scale, different.
- [Wix added $150M in revenue, then fired 1,000 people to afford it.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/wix-added-150m-in-revenue-then-fired-1000-people-to): This week it is firing 1,000 people to afford it. Revenue up 14% to $541 million. A $34 million profit last year turned into a $57 million loss. Cash flow down.

CEO was about to fire the whole sales team. The reps had been screaming the actual problem for months. Nobody listened. Here's what happened..
- [Why does this bus cost 15x more in Boston than in Beijing? It is not quality.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/why-does-this-bus-cost-15x-more-in-boston-than): Same size, same purpose, same number of passengers. One costs about $80,000. The other can hit $1.2 million. Quality? Nope.. The American bus is not 15x.

Growing 50%+ a year. What's worth noting isn't the price tag. It's the timing. D-Fend didn't pivot. The world's pain caught up.

I've watched.

With a decision. One player. One moment. And a system that took decades to build starts coming apart. This is how systems actually end..

They buy emotionally, then justify it.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/buyers-dont-buy-logically-they-buy-emotionally-then-justify-it): Then they use logic to explain what they already decided. Trump gets this better than anyone. One sentence. And suddenly everyone feels something. Rage.

- [Aston Martin is betting everything on one car. So are most founders.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/aston-martin-is-betting-everything-on-one-car-so-are): Aston Martin is betting its future on one car. The Valhalla. $1M per unit. 999 units. ~$1B potential revenue. Not just a car. A financial recovery plan. Now.
- [A partnership where only one side delivers is not a partnership. It is a liability.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/a-partnership-where-only-one-side-delivers-is-not-a): It's a liability. I've been there personally. Signed agreements that looked great on paper. Shook hands with people who had the right title, the right network.
- [France cut off arms to Israel. Israel's answer: build it ourselves. A lesson in suppliers.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/france-cut-off-arms-to-israel-israels-answer-build-it): Israel's response? Find better suppliers. Build more. Depend less. Israel's Ministry of Defense just announced it's halting all defense procurement from France.
- [71% of Israeli startups say the war hurt fundraising. 90% are still building.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/71-of-israeli-startups-say-the-war-hurt-fundraising-90): And yet they're still here. Still building. Still pushing. That number isn't a eulogy. It's a testament. A survey of 637 Israeli tech companies published today.
- [UVision is heading to Nasdaq at $3.5B. It never had a sales problem.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/uvision-is-heading-to-nasdaq-at-35b-it-never-had): An Israeli drone company. That's not funding. That's a land grab. They're not raising capital..

Got this message today: We have all the problems you talk about. This is not rare. I.

- [Honda burned $16B. Not a failed startup, a failure to read the market in time.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/honda-burned-16b-not-a-failed-startup-a-failure-to): Not a bad product. Honda. They canceled multiple EV models they had already invested heavily in. Why? Because reality didn't match the plan: EV demand in key.

Can you actually speed the process up?](https://talpaperin.com/blog/know-like-trust-buy-can-you-actually-speed-the-process): That's the process your prospect is going through. Starting from not knowing you (or ever hearing about your product) and all the way to placing an order. Each.
- [Could you sell spoiled milk to a furniture store? It is the wrong question.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/could-you-sell-spoiled-milk-to-a-furniture-store-it): Let's see how good of a Salesperson you are: Do you think you'd be able to sell spoiled milk to a furniture store? If not - what if I'd bring you world's best.
- [A Tel Aviv traffic jam is a four-step lesson in building a business.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/a-tel-aviv-traffic-jam-is-a-four-step-lesson): This is a traffic jam in Tel Aviv, Israel (due to a protest and a road block). THIS is how you create a business: 1. Identify a need. 2. Come up with a.
- [How to reach your target and avoid the fog: build a plan with linked milestones.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/how-to-reach-your-target-and-avoid-the-fog-build): Very easy. 1. You build a plan with milestones. 2. Each milestone has several desired outcomes. AND 3. Each milestone has a reference to a desired outcome of.
- [Robert Herjavec's 5 tips for entrepreneurs, and the one I would pick.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/robert-herjavecs-5-tips-for-entrepreneurs-and-the-one-i): Just in case you DON'T have 3 minutes to listen to Shark Tank's Robert Herjavec (and you SHOULD!), here they are: 1. Look for a problem - and solve it! 2. Ask.
- [Your marketing wrapper is not a distraction. It is the first thing buyers see.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/your-marketing-wrapper-is-not-a-distraction-it-is-the): There is this new theory about the "bells and whistles" or the "eye candy" of your marketing efforts. I mean all the "wrapper" in which you cover your product.
- [A $200M Israeli exit proves the oldest sales rule: go where nobody else will.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/a-200m-israeli-exit-proves-the-oldest-sales-rule-go): Amazon Web Services just bought an Israeli Start-up, CloudEndure for the above sum. What's the lesson we learn? Since the beginning of time, Sales and Business.
- [In-store product placement is how brick and mortar beats online.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/in-store-product-placement-is-how-brick-and-mortar-beats): You can use your store and your location to promote ANY brand - and not just as a POP (Point of Purchase). - [Sales is storytelling. Even a boring product has a better story to tell.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/sales-is-storytelling-even-a-boring-product-has-a-better): Now, you might think that your product or service are borrrring..... and they might be. But there is always a BETTER way to tell the story about and behind.
- [Real leaders improvise. They deviate from the plan and use what is in the room.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/real-leaders-improvise-they-deviate-from-the-plan-and-use): Deviate from the plan. Use what's available right there, make new plans, tactics and strategies. Look around - and come up with new usages for your assets.
- [How many questions does it take to close a C-level deal? The data has an answer.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/how-many-questions-does-it-take-to-close-a-c): But how many? Is it 10? Is it 20? Well, the answer is surprising - and it's based on 30k sales conversations, examined by an AI. This is true ONLY for C-Level.
- [Sales is like the movies: no script, no blockbuster.](https://talpaperin.com/blog/sales-is-like-the-movies-no-script-no-blockbuster): Preferably one made by a professional. Than both need to have some actors to play it out - and they better be talented. Have that? Got yourself a blockbuster!.
